steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a subject-verb agreement question (Standard English Conventions). The sentence structure is “The radiation that [blank] during the decay of radioactive atomic nuclei is known as gamma radiation.” The blank fills the verb in the relative clause “that [blank].” The subject of that clause is “radiation,” which is singular.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "occurs": Singular present-tense verb; agrees with the singular subject “radiation.” ✓
  • "have occurred": Plural verb (“have” requires a plural subject); violates subject-verb agreement with the singular “radiation.” ✗
  • "occur": Plural base form; used with plural subjects or with “I”, not with singular third-person “radiation.” Violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • "are occurring": Plural progressive form (“are” requires a plural subject); violates subject-verb agreement with the singular “radiation.” ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
